name: Paneer Tikka
cooking_tips: Marinate paneer for at least 2 hours for maximum flavor absorption. Grill on high heat for char.

name: Amritsari Fish Fry
cooking_tips: Double fry the fish: once on medium heat to cook through, then on high heat for crispiness.

name: Hara Bhara Kebab
cooking_tips: Blanch spinach and squeeze out all water to prevent kebabs from breaking.

name: Chicken Malai Tikka
cooking_tips: Use hung curd and fresh cream for the silkiest texture. Don't overcook to keep juicy.

name: Dahi Bhalla
cooking_tips: Soak fried vadas in warm salted water for 20 mins to make them super soft.

name: Samosa Plate
cooking_tips: Fry on low heat for a long time to get the outer crust perfectly crispy and flaky.

name: Tandoori Gobi
cooking_tips: Blanch cauliflower florets before marinating to ensure they cook evenly in the tandoor.

name: Seekh Kebab
cooking_tips: Keep the minced meat very cold while mixing to help the fat bind the kebab together.

name: Butter Chicken
cooking_tips: Add butter and cream at the very end and turn off heat immediately to prevent splitting.

name: Dal Makhani
cooking_tips: Simmer on low heat overnight for the authentic creamy texture. Smoke with charcoal if possible.

name: Chole Bhature
cooking_tips: Add a tea bag while boiling chickpeas to get that dark, authentic color.

name: Malai Kofta
cooking_tips: Do not add koftas to the gravy while boiling. Serve gravy over koftas just before eating.

name: Mutton Rogan Josh
cooking_tips: Use Ratan Jot (Alkanet root) infused oil for the signature bright red color without chili heat.

name: Shahi Paneer
cooking_tips: Use cashew paste and saffron for the royal 'Shahi' flavor profile and creamy consistency.

name: Kadai Chicken
cooking_tips: Roast and grind coriander seeds and dry red chilies fresh for the Kadai masala.

name: Palak Paneer
cooking_tips: Do not cover the pan after adding spinach to retain the vibrant green color.

name: Sarson da Saag
cooking_tips: Add a tablespoon of cornmeal (makki atta) to thicken the saag and give it a silky texture.

name: Baingan Bharta
cooking_tips: Roast eggplants on an open flame until the skin chars completely for that essential smoky flavor.

name: Chicken Biryani
cooking_tips: Seal the pot with dough (Dum) to trap steam and aroma for at least 20 minutes on low heat.

name: Vegetable Biryani
cooking_tips: Fry onions until golden brown (Barista) and layer generous amounts for sweetness and crunch.

name: Jeera Rice
cooking_tips: Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes before cooking for long, separate grains.

name: Steamed Rice
cooking_tips: Add a few drops of lemon juice to the boiling water to keep the rice grains white and separate.

name: Garlic Naan
cooking_tips: Apply garlic butter immediately after taking it out of the tandoor while it's still hot.

name: Butter Naan
cooking_tips: Rest the dough for at least 1-2 hours to ensure the naan becomes soft and fluffy.

name: Tandoori Roti
cooking_tips: Keep the hydration of the dough high for softer rotis.

name: Makki di Roti
cooking_tips: Use hot water to knead the cornmeal dough to make it pliable and easy to flatten.

name: Lachha Paratha
cooking_tips: Create as many pleats as possible and use plenty of ghee between layers for max flakiness.

name: Green Salad
cooking_tips: Keep vegetables in ice water for 15 minutes before serving for extra crunch.

name: Boondi Raita
cooking_tips: Soak boondi in warm water for 5 minutes and squeeze before adding to yogurt to reduce oil.

name: Papadum
cooking_tips: Roast on an open flame for a smokier flavor compared to frying.

name: Gulab Jamun
cooking_tips: Fry on very low heat so that the center cooks before the outside burns.

name: Gajar ka Halwa
cooking_tips: Use red Delhi carrots (winter carrots) for the best natural sweetness and color.

name: Ras Malai
cooking_tips: Squeeze the chenna balls gently after boiling but before soaking in milk to absorb flavor.

name: Kulfi
cooking_tips: Reduce the milk to 1/3rd of its original volume slowly for the authentic gritty texture.

name: Mango Lassi
cooking_tips: Use chilled yogurt and mango pulp to avoid adding ice, which dilutes the flavor.

name: Sweet Lassi
cooking_tips: Whisk by hand using a wooden churner (madhani) for a frothy top layer.

name: Salted Lassi
cooking_tips: Roasted cumin powder freshly ground adds a much better aroma than pre-ground cumin.

name: Masala Chai
cooking_tips: Boil ginger and spices in water first before adding milk to prevent milk from curdling.

name: Fresh Lime Soda
cooking_tips: Muddle a few fresh mint leaves in the glass for a refreshing twist.

name: Soft Drink
cooking_tips: Serve with a slice of lemon and plenty of ice.
